english: name of an ancient Chinese ruler

etymology.description: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 虫 (insect) + phonetic 兀 (sound) -> related to 虺 (a kind of snake) -> later used for the legendary ruler 禹
etymology.interpretation: The character 禹 combines the insect radical 虫 (chóng, "insect") for association with creatures and the phonetic element 兀 (wù, "cut-off foot"). Originally part of words relating to snakes or creatures, it was borrowed for its sound to denote Emperor Yu, a semi-mythical figure known for taming nature, connecting the character to a legendary ruler rather than an animal form.

examples.chinese: 大禹治水时(大禹治水時)，展示了非凡的才能(展示了非凡的才能)。
examples.pinyin: Dà Yǔ zhì shuǐ shí，zhǎnshì le fēifán de cáinéng.
examples.english: When Yu the Great controlled the floods, he displayed extraordinary talent.

examples.chinese: 禹是中国历史上的传奇人物(禹是中國曆史上的傳奇人物)。
examples.pinyin: Yǔ shì Zhōngguó lìshǐ shàng de chuánqí rénwù.
examples.english: Yu is a legendary figure in Chinese history.

examples.chinese: 传说禹花了十三年时间(傳說禹花了十三年時間)，才治理好黄河水患(才治理好黃河水患)。
examples.pinyin: Chuánshuō yǔ huā le shí sān nián shíjiān，cái zhìlǐ hǎo Huánghé shuǐhuàn.
examples.english: Legend has it that Yu spent thirteen years to successfully control the Yellow River floods.
